assertions.description: SubtleCrypto API is exposed on the window.crypto object.
crypto.subtle.generateKey is a function used to generate cryptographic key pairs.
crypto.subtle.deriveBits is a function used to derive bits for key agreement.
crypto.subtle.deriveKey is a function used to derive keys for key agreement.
WebCrypto supports generating keys for the X25519 algorithm for application-layer key agreement; this is adjacent to, but not proof of, TLS hybrid KEM negotiation.
assertions.test: (async () => { try { await crypto.subtle.generateKey({ name: 'X25519' }, false, ['deriveKey', 'deriveBits']); return true; } catch { return false; } })()

assertions.description: PerformanceNavigationTiming exposes nextHopProtocol as an application/network protocol token such as h2, h3, or http/1.1; it is not the selected TLS NamedGroup or KEM.
The current navigation's nextHopProtocol value does not expose X25519Kyber768, ML-KEM, or another TLS KEM name.
assertions.test: (() => { const nav = performance.getEntriesByType('navigation')[0]; const value = nav && typeof nav.nextHopProtocol === 'string' ? nav.nextHopProtocol : ''; return !/(kyber|ml-?kem|x25519|kem|namedgroup)/i.test(value); })()

assertions.description: The document is loaded in a secure context (HTTPS or localhost), which is required for WebCrypto APIs.
